::: {.tikz-desc data-node="sdata"}
##### Private data 

Sender's sensitive data, held privately and never shared.

$$x \in \mathsf{X}$$
:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="mech"}
##### Mechanism

A (deterministic or randomised) mechanism.

$$M: (\mathsf{X}, \mathcal{T}) \rightarrow [0,1]$$

Markov kernel w.r.t. measurable space $(\mathsf{T}, \mathcal{T})$.

:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="sstat"}
##### Statistic

Generated from mechanism and shared with Receiver.

$$T \sim M(x, \cdot)$$
:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="strans"}
##### Privacy guarantee

Shared with Receiver for transparency, so that the privacy of $M$ can be verified externally.

<br>

_[Assumption 1](#assumption-1) dictates that the privacy guarantee can not depend on $x$ directly. This prevents information leakage about the data through transparency. As such, communication of the guarantee to Receiver does not affect the data-posterior._

:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="spriv"}
##### Privacy function

The privacy function encodes how Receiver's decision affect the privacy of Sender.

<br>

_Under the data-posterior $Q_T$, with Receiver's optimal decision $d^{Q_T}$, the realised privacy for Sender is_

$$
\rho(d^{Q_T},x).
$$

:::


::: {.tikz-desc data-node="adata"}
##### Data-prior

The receiver's assumed prior over the data is

$$Q \in \mathcal{P}(\mathsf{X}).$$

<br>

_In [Assumption 5](#assumption-5) we have a class of prior distributions $\mathcal{Q}_x \subset \mathcal{P}(\mathsf{X})$ for which $Q \in \mathcal{Q}_x$ and Sender assesses the worst case._

:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="apost"}
##### Data-posterior

Receiver's posterior given the release of $T$ is 

$$Q_T = Q(\,\cdot \mid T),$$
which will depend on the chosen mechanism $M$.

<br>

_By [Assumption 1](#assumption-1), $Q_T$ does not depend on $\mathfrak{C}$._

:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="aact"}
##### Receiver decision

Receiver's decision is formed by posterior $Q_T$ and loss $\ell$.

$$
d^{Q_T} \in \arg\inf_{d \in \mathcal{D}} \mathbb{E}_{z \sim Q_T}[ \ell(d,z)]
$$
<br>

_[Assumption 3](#assumption-3) dictates that $\ell = \rho$._

:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="aloss"}
##### Receiver loss

Receiver's loss function for decision making.

<br>

_[Assumption 3](#assumption-3) dictates that $\ell = \rho$. In other words, Receiver directly targets Sender's notion of privacy._

:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="statbox"}
##### Sender

Custodian of sensitive data $x$.  Chooses $M$ to persuade Receiver to make decisions with limited impact on privacy. 

<br>

_Sender conducts a robust assessment of privacy. These are formalised in [Assumption 4](#assumption-4) and [Assumption 5](#assumption-5)._

:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="advbox"}
##### Receiver

Receiver is a potential adversary who will make a decision after observing output $T \sim M(x,\cdot)$.

<br>

_Receiver is Bayes rational by [Assumption 2](#assumption-2). Therefore, they hold a prior $Q$ over (unknown) data $x$, and make decisions with respect to some loss function $\ell$._


:::
